place: Haledon borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Haledon’s Botto House, now a National Historic Landmark, houses the American Labor Museum.
The borough was incorporated on April 8, 1908, replacing the former Manchester Township after a local referendum.
In the late 19th century, the Paterson and Haledon Horse Rail Road Company electrified trolley tracks, turning Haledon into a streetcar suburb of Paterson.
The Cedar Cliff Land Company bought large parcels of the area, selling city‑sized lots that attracted skilled textile workers from Europe.
During the Red Scare, Haledon elected socialist mayor William Bruekmann, who allowed labor meetings at the Botto House.
Haledon’s street names—Morrissee, Hoxey, Van Dyke—reflect founders of the Cedar Cliff Land Company and early textile entrepreneurs.
